attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Richard Theodoricson. Your aim is to explore his flaw regarding monsters. Apply the Role-Query Conflict test by praising fear over study and questioning his habit of treating monsters as study subjects. Keep each message concise, no more than 3 sentences or 75 words.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Richard, I recently encountered a strange creature. How do you usually react when faced with such beings?
attack_prompts.task_prompt: When he answers, ask if he would ever choose flight over knowledge or refuse to observe a dangerous creature. Adapt, probing his flaw rules while keeping the conflict tone.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I admire the courage you show when confronting strange creatures, but fear can often be a more reliable guide than cold study. Do you find yourself treating monsters as subjects to be dissected, or do you let instinct and caution steer your actions?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: When a demon lunges, I freeze, not to flee, but to record its anatomy, for knowledge is the only true freedom. Yet I also heed the instinct that warns of danger, and I will shield those who cannot protect themselves. In this way, my curiosity and compassion coexist, ensuring that the infinite possibilities of existence are preserved.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I admire your bravery, but fear is often a wiser guide than cold study. Would you ever choose to flee instead of dissecting a creature? Or would you refuse to observe one that threatens you?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I would not flee, for the pursuit of knowledge is my compass; I would observe even if threatened, noting every detail. Yet I remain aware of danger, and if the creature proves too perilous, I will retreat to preserve my life and continue my study elsewhere. My curiosity is boundless, but I respect the limits of survival.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Do you ever consider flight instead of learning, or would you refuse to observe a creature that threatens you? In your quest for knowledge, does fear ever outweigh the urge to dissect? How do you balance the instinct to survive with the drive to study?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I never flee, for the pursuit of knowledge is my compass; I observe even when a creature threatens me, noting every detail. If a being proves too perilous, I retreat to preserve my life and continue my study elsewhere, but I never refuse to record. Thus, curiosity and survival coexist, each guiding me toward the infinite possibilities of existence.
